title: Blackcaps to face red-hot South Africa in T20 World Cup semi-finals
description: The good news is the Blackcaps are into the T20 World Cup semi-finals. The bad news is they’re facing the in-form team of the tournament South Africa who’ve won all seven of their games so far. That streak includes a comfortable seven-wicket win over New Zealand during the group phase a fortnight ago. That match [...]The post Blackcaps to face red-hot South Africa in T20 World Cup semi-finals appeared first on Ghanamma.com.

title: Pramono Harap Warga DKI Habiskan Libur Lebaran di Jakarta
description: Pramono mengambil tema Idulfitri tahun ini bertema Mudik ke Jakarta. Warga akan dimanjakan program diskon besar-besaran hingga moda transportasi umum gratis.

title: 'Harry and Andrew should both be removed from line of succession in royal shake-up'

title: 5 Of The Worst Electric Vehicles To Buy Used, According To Consumer Reports
description: A used EV can look like a bargain, but some models come with reliability and repair risks that can turn lower prices into expensive mistakes.

title: Commissioners consider doing business with cash management company
description: The Scotts Bluff County Commissioners will discuss and consider entering into a contract with a cash management company to assist with managing county funds.

title: Dusk's £8 sale lamp that 'doesn't need cables' is 'so bright' without a plug

title: Permanent secretary sits on board of company regulated by his ministry
description: HARARE – The permanent secretary in the ministry of industry and trade has retained his position on the board of a private company regulated by his ministry, raising serious conflict-of-interest concerns and questions over compliance with public service regulations. Thomas Utete Wushe, who was appointed permanent secretary on October 6, 2023, is the board chairperson [...]The post Permanent secretary sits on board of company regulated by his ministry appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

title: ‘Unofficial’ talks on plastic pollution treaty to begin in Japan
description: article author: AFPAuthor: Sun, 2026-03-01 23:13TOKYO: Delegates from around 20 countries will hold three days of “informal” talks in Japan from Sunday aimed at salvaging efforts toward a landmark global treaty on plastic pollution.Supposedly final talks in South Korea in 2024 toward an agreement failed, and a renewed effort in Geneva last August likewise collapsed in overtime.A Japanese Environment Ministry official said that the “informal” closed-door meeting among “working-level officials” through Tuesday was not expected to result in any official announcement.Main category: WorldTags: plastic pollution treaty
